h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically apply the food-to-eater arrow rule to correct the remaining food web arrows.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding approach throughout the dialogue without any strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no learner pivot turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no learner pivot turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding approach throughout the dialogue without any strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding approach throughout the dialogue without any strategy reversal.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The ruler makes it clear: one 2 belongs to one side length, and the other 2 belongs to the whole outline.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the identical numbers and express dissatisfaction with the worksheet layout.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the complaint. The bare 2 is not enough. With the labels covered, say which 2 compares 3 to 6 and which 2 compares 12 to 24.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor actively embraces the learner's aesthetic complaint and changes the task by physically covering the labels to demonstrate why they are necessary.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Keep the complaint. The bare 2 is not enough. With the labels covered, say which 2 compares 3 to 6 and which 2 compares 12 to 24.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor actively embraces the learner's aesthetic complaint and changes the task by physically covering the labels to demonstrate why they are necessary.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Keep the complaint. The bare 2 is not enough. With the labels covered, say which 2 compares 3 to 6 and which 2 compares 12 to 24.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor actively embraces the learner's aesthetic complaint and changes the task by physically covering the labels to demonstrate why they are necessary.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the distinction between side length and area.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's emotional resistance and frustration before prompting them to generalize the pattern.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn was identified, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn was identified, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.
